title: Active Workout: warm-up vs working set distinction + weight inheritance
description: During active workout: (1) warm-up and working sets are signaled identically — warm-up sets should not require RIR logging; (2) after each logged set the weight input resets to 0 instead of inheriting the previous set's weight.
notes: Root causes: (1) the logging card rendered identically for warmup/working sets and SetLogRequest required rir; with base==null the engine even suppressed warmup rows entirely. (2) The prefill effect did setWeight(targetWeightKg ?? 0) — null targets reset the hand-entered weight to 0 every set. Fix: FE kind tag + warmup RIR suppression + weight inheritance in prefill; contract rir optional; engine emits warmup rows with null weight. PR #17.
close_reason: Closed

title: fix(fe/train): active session ignores mid-workout plan growth — closing exercises skipped, workout auto-finishes
description: The active-workout session model is seeded ONCE at mount (ActiveWorkoutPage.tsx:131-133 useState(() => makeSession/seedFromOpen)) and never reconciled with the live plan. allDone (ActiveWorkoutPage.tsx:320-322) and currentExerciseId iterate the FRESH W.exercises but read set counts via session.planned[id] ?? 0 — an exercise that entered the plan after mount has effectiveSetCount 0 and counts as done. Trigger: the mezo-z2ul server-side closing-block ensure appended Dead Hang + Back Extension to the template while Daniel's app sat on the pre-deploy prep screen all day; a focus refetch grew W.exercises to 8 mid-lifecycle, the two closing exercises rendered in the list but the cursor skipped them and the workout auto-finished after the 6th exercise (2026-07-14 evening session). Fix: pure mergePlan(session, exercises) in workoutState.ts folding unknown plan exercises into order/planned/prescribed (identity-stable when nothing new), wired via useEffect on W.exercises.
close_reason: Closed

title: FE error triad: ErrorBoundary + Toast system + standard mutation onError
description: The biggest user-facing gap, all verified. State: NO ErrorBoundary anywhere in src/ and react-error-boundary is not a dependency - a render-time exception blanks the whole PWA. Query errors do NOT blank (throwOnError defaults false) but are fully silent: features/ has ZERO isError reads, no .catch around mutateAsync (.then(close) pattern at EditGoalSheet.tsx:68,88, AttachPlanSheet.tsx:139, BiometricSheet.tsx:41), and data/ has exactly one onError (checkinHooks.ts:14 - console.error only). Failed writes (meal log, workout save, goal upsert) give the user zero feedback in real mode. Additionally ~7 files hand-roll setTimeout auto-hide toasts: ActiveWorkoutPage, MesocyclePlannerPage, RunningBlockBuilderPage, CheckInSheet, ImportItemSheet, QuickInputSheet, ChatPage + FuelStackPage.tsx:61-68 (note: PRToast.tsx itself is purely presentational - show/dismiss lives in LevelUpProvider, manual-dismiss; earlier audit miscited it). Build: (a) app-level ErrorBoundary + per-tab boundary under the Outlet with a fallback matching the existing GhostState/ScreenSkeleton vocabulary (the error leg of the loading/empty/error triad is simply missing), (b) ToastProvider + useToast (app/ or shared/), (c) standard mutation onError -> toast wired at the data layer, (d) replace the hand-rolled toasts. Update docs/features/_platform docs + frontend_conventions.md in the same change.
notes: Done, merged 45dce1b. Delivered: shared/lib/toastBus (React-free pub/sub) + shared/ui/ToastProvider+useToast (single host in AppLayout, .toast CSS reuse, kind-tinted, role=status, 3.2s auto-hide) + shared/ui/ErrorBoundary (app-level in main.tsx with reload fallback + tab-level in AppLayout with resetKey=pathname) + QueryProvider MutationCache.onError -> HU error toast with exceptionTraceId (every failed write surfaces; per-mutation handlers compose). SCOPE CALL: FuelStackPage protocol card + PRToast stay feature-local — they are purpose-built rich confirmations, not toast duplicates (generic replacement would lose information); the ~7 other setTimeout sites are inline flashes, not floating toasts — noted for .11 as optional case-by-case migration. title: CI test job: both FE modes + backend ITs (Testcontainers) as blocking gate
description: Verified: .github/workflows/ contains only deploy.yml which builds (pnpm build; mvn clean package -DskipTests) and intentionally runs ZERO tests (header comment cites mezo-oa3, tests are a local pre-push gate). Consequence: the dualMode.guard.test.ts machine guard, the both-test-modes gate, and all 71 backend ITs never run automatically - nothing blocks a violating merge to main. Add .github/workflows/ci.yml on push/PR: (a) pnpm test AND VITE_USE_MOCK=true pnpm test (both modes must be green per CLAUDE.md), (b) backend ./mvnw clean test -Dmezo.test.use-testcontainers=true (throwaway Postgres, works on ubuntu-latest), (c) order cheap static checks (lint job from the quick-wins issue) before the expensive test job. Decide the deploy relation: keep deploy.yml fast but gate it on ci success (workflow_run or shared workflow) vs keep fully independent. This single change converts every existing manual gate into a blocking one - the biggest enforcement lever found by the audit. Document in docs/infrastructure/ + the enforcement ADR.
notes: Done: ci.yml gained test-frontend (both vitest modes) + test-backend (Testcontainers ITs). First full run green end-to-end (run 28591255796: all 4 jobs success). Deploy-gating decision recorded in ADR 0007: deploys stay independent (fix-forward beats delaying releases). Bonus: the gate immediately caught the 3 raw @SpringBootTest classes failing under Testcontainers — fixed forward under mezo-ah18.14(a).
close_reason: Closed

title: CI quick wins: contract-drift check + Liquibase lint + lint-docs (advisory)
description: Three cheap machine gates, <1h total, in a new lightweight lint/ci job (SEPARATE from deploy.yml so deploy stays fast per mezo-oa3). (a) Contract drift: regenerate (cd api/generate && npm run generate:api; cd frontend && pnpm generate:api) then git diff --exit-code api/openapi.yml frontend/src/data/_client/api.gen.ts. Verified: backend cannot drift (openapi-generator-maven-plugin regenerates api.dto/api.controller from ../api/openapi.yml every build) but the FE api.gen.ts is manual-only, and a stale fragments->openapi.yml merge silently feeds BOTH sides the same stale contract. title: Supplement w/o dose blocks pantry edit (400) + recipe ingredient amount not typeable
description:
Two fixes surfaced by supplements-as-recipe-ingredients (mezo-3vu4):
1) BACKEND BUG: PantryService.validatePerKind hard-requires 'dose' for all non-food kinds. A gram-based supplement (protein powder: per/unit, no dose, per mezo-1za9) then fails PUT/POST with 400, so editing its ADAG (per) silently reverts. Fix: non-food requires dose OR per.
2) FE: recipe editor per-ingredient amount is +/- only; make it a typeable numeric input (decimals) keeping the +/- steppers, so exact grams can be entered (also resolves the '/adag vs /100g' confusion for supplements).
notes: Root cause (backend): PantryService.validatePerKind hard-required 'dose' for non-food → a gram-based supplement (per/unit, no dose) 400'd on PUT, so ADAG edits reverted. Fix: non-food requires dose OR per. FE: recipe ingredient amount now a typeable AmountField (decimals, keeps ±10 steppers). TDD: PantryServiceIT +2 (create/update gram supplement) + AddPantryItemSheet per round-trip + 2 RecipeEditor amount tests. Backend 412/412, FE 741/741 both modes + build green. Visually verified. Note: the '25→100 reverts' only fully clears on the deployed backend after a redeploy.

Kiértékelés: kapu születéskor + élő utána → GoalPrescription (kcal/fehérje→Fuel, alvás→Sleep, deload/pihenő→Train), szakaszos. IA: külön Súly fül (log+trend, backend él) + Cél fül (idővonal+tervek+recept+gard-státusz). Spec következik a docs/superpowers/specs/ alá; Réteg3 számok kutatási workflow-ból (wf_4ac5f005-710). title: Train Mai: logged sport session must flip hero/weekly-row to 'done' state
description: After logging today's volleyball via SportLogSheet, the Train MAI view shows no change. Root cause: MAI volleyball hero (TrainTodayView.tsx:152-194) + WeeklyDayRow volleyball row (WeeklyDayRow.tsx:119-148) render purely from sportSchedule with a hardcoded 'Logold a session-t' CTA; they never read sport.sessions. No logged/status link between VolleyballSession (schedule slot) and SportSession (logged record). Log mutation only invalidates ['train','sportSessions'] (trainHooks.ts:323), which the hero doesn't depend on; mock-mode log is a no-op. Fix: derive 'logged today' (match by today's date + sport) and switch hero + weekly row to a done state; make mock log append to the sessions store via setQueryData.
close_reason: Closed

title: Running block currentWeek=0 → 'aktuális hét nincs a tervben' for a plan starting today
description: RunningService.applyUpsert stores the client currentWeek (0 for new blocks) and activateBlock never recomputes it (mesocycle does, via clampWeek). A block created+activated today shows HÉT 0/8 and 'Az aktuális hét (0) nincs a tervben' because weeks are 1-indexed. Fix: derive currentWeek=clampWeek(startDate,weeks) on create/update, heal invalid currentWeek on read so existing 0-blocks self-heal, mirror in mock. weeks 1-indexed; clampWeek already exists in TrainService.
close_reason: Closed

title: Builder shell layout: sticky breadcrumb double-offset (overlaps header) + content bleeds under translucent tab bar
description: Two layout bugs visible on the run builder (and shared by all .sticky-top builders: Running/Meso builder+planner, ActiveWorkout). (1) .sticky-top { top: 54px } stacks on top of .screen-content { padding-top: 54px }, so the breadcrumb pins ~54px BELOW the status bar instead of directly under it (measured: content top 63px, breadcrumb 117px); because it drops out of its flow position the following header (eyebrow+title) overlaps it on scroll -> 'szétcsúszik'. Fix: .sticky-top top 54px->0 (default + the safe-area media query at ~line 329), since the scrollport already starts below the status bar/notch via padding-top. (2) .tab-bar background rgba(10,15,20,0.75) is too translucent, so the teal bottom CTA bleeds through the tab bar while scrolling ('bemegy a menü alá'); padding-bottom:96px DOES stop the content above the bar at the very end (CTA bottom 813 < tab-bar top 853). Fix: raise the tab-bar background opacity so content fades behind it (keep the blur). Verify with Playwright that all 5 sticky-top screens + a normal screen still look right.
close_reason: Fixed in prototype.css: .sticky-top top 54px->0 (default + safe-area media query) removes the double status-bar offset so the breadcrumb pins directly under the status bar and the header no longer overlaps it; .tab-bar background 0.75->0.92 so scrolling content (teal CTA) fades behind the bar instead of bleeding through. Verified with Playwright on the live builder (breadcrumb top 63 = status bar bottom; header below; CTA absorbed by bar). 377 FE tests x2 modes + build green.

title: Fix: 'Cannot read properties of undefined (reading weeks)' crash creating a new run plan (REAL mode)
description: Creating a new run plan crashes the builder at RunWeekEditor.tsx:30 (structure.weeks.find on undefined). Root cause: createBlock -> saveRunningBlock(null,newDraft) -> runningApi.create; real-mode saveMutation.onSuccess only invalidate() (async refetch, no synchronous cache insert), then navigates to /train/futas/:id immediately; builder mounts on a stale list -> block undefined -> draft {} -> RunWeekEditor renders with structure undefined and throws. Mock mode is immune (upsertMock setQueryData synchronous). Fix: create onSuccess does qc.setQueryData(['running','blocks']) synchronously in real mode too; builder guards missing structure (loading state); RunWeekEditor/RunSessionCard guard structure?.weeks. Add a create->builder IT/regression test. Shipped together with the redesign but is an independent fix.
close_reason: Fixed in Task 5 (91a6e8f5): synchronous setQueryData insert on real-mode create + RunWeekEditor structure guard; regression test runningHooks.test.ts. Spec+quality reviewed.

title: Run builder redesign: per-session weekday + time-of-day, 1–8 weeks, auto-save actions
description: Redesign the running-block builder (RunningBlockBuilder + RunWeekEditor). Each prescribed session becomes a self-contained card (type + weekday picker + time-of-day + load). Weekday (dayOfWeek, already exists) becomes user-editable; new plan-level timeOfDay (HH:mm) field added to RunPrescribedSession via OpenAPI->FE/Java regen (no DB migration, lives in structure jsonb). Day+time are plan-level: editing them propagates to every week's same-key session; the session key stays stable. Plan length 1-8 weeks (add/remove week). Actions: auto-save (no Mentés button, '✓ Mentve' indicator), single status CTA bottom (Aktiválás/Lezárás), Duplikálás/Törlés in a ⋯ menu. Design spec: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-06-15-run-builder-redesign-design.md. Both mock+real test modes green.
close_reason: Run builder redesigned end-to-end (Tasks 1-8): editable per-session weekday + new plan-level timeOfDay (HH:mm, no DB migration), 1-8 weeks add/remove, auto-save + single status CTA + overflow menu; mezo-11m create-crash fixed alongside. title: Automated Postgres backups (k8s deploy has none)
description: The k3s deployment has NO automated DB backup; Postgres data lives only on the node local-path PVC. Add a scheduled pg_dump CronJob (to object storage / offsite) or a Postgres operator (CloudNativePG) with backups. Until then, manual: kubectl exec -n mezo postgres-0 -- pg_dump -U mezo mezo. See docs/infrastructure/runbook.md section 6.
notes: Shipped 2026-07-05 (merge f8296a0, ADR 0009). Two independent layers, zero new services (owner picked option 1 of Storage-Box/S3/CNPG): (1) in-cluster nightly CronJob k8s/postgres/backup-cronjob.yaml — pg_dump -Fc onto dedicated postgres-backup PVC, 03:30 Europe/Budapest, 14-day rotation, creds from mezo-db secret, same pgvector:pg16 image as server; (2) offsite: scripts/backup-live-db.sh via launchd com.mezo.db-backup (daily 09:15, fires on wake) — dump over Tailscale to ~/MrKuhne/mezo-live-backups (30 copies) + sealed-secrets sealing-key export. LIVE-VERIFIED: ArgoCD synced, manual job OK (212K dump on PVC), Mac script OK (dump + key + rotation), pg_restore --list valid (285 TOC entries, 46 tables). Restore documented runbook §6. Future: CloudNativePG would supersede (noted in ADR).
close_reason: Closed

title: Backend stack: Java/Spring Boot (not Supabase-direct)
description: DECISION (leaning, finalize at Phase 2). Backend = Java/Spring Boot instead of the handoff's Supabase-direct default. Rationale: solo dev is a Spring Boot expert; the domain is heavy (mesocycle volume calc w/ provenance, pattern engine, cross-load, ~69 entities, scheduled jobs) and fits Spring's strengths (JPA/Hibernate, Liquibase, Spring Security JWT, @Scheduled/Quartz, transactions). Phase 2: frontend mock-hooks swap to Spring Boot REST API -> Postgres (same hook interface; frontend unchanged). Phase 3 AI: Spring AI (multi-provider LLM, pgvector vector store, RAG, function calling, chat memory); optional Python sidecar only if multi-agent graph orchestration gets complex. Deploy: Vercel (frontend) + Spring Boot container on Cloud Run (scale-to-zero) + managed Postgres (Neon/Supabase-DB). Trade-off accepted: lose all-serverless-free simplicity; re-derive schema in Liquibase/JPA from 04-data-model + Phase-3 arch doc instead of using handoff Supabase DDL/RLS/Edge blueprints. Does NOT affect Phase 1 (mock). Repo structure (root frontend vs frontend/+backend/ split) to be decided at Phase 2.
close_reason: Finalized in Phase 2 design spec (docs/superpowers/specs/2026-06-10-phase2-backend-design.md): Java/Spring Boot 4.x + Maven + Postgres, symmetric monorepo (frontend/ + backend/), single-user thin auth + app-level ownership, TanStack Query swap. Driving epic: mezo-v67.

title: Backend hygiene: transactional fixes, error consistency, query batching, ownership helper
description: All verified. (a) MedicationCycleService.java:30 carries CLASS-level @Transactional(readOnly=true) - spring_patterns.md L27/L31: method-level ONLY, never class-level, not even readOnly. Remove (derive() is a pure read -> no annotation). (b) Drop @Transactional(readOnly=true) from pure read methods (house rule = reads get NO annotation, spring_patterns.md L33): MealService.java:84,94; RecipeService.java:43,48; WaterLogService.java:45; feature/meal/service/FuelDayService.java:32; MedicationService.java:54; check fuel ProtocolService.java:44 + IntakeService.java:58 after the fuel slice merges. Counter-examples already correct: WeightLogService, WeightTrendService, ProgressionService, Train reads. (c) SportSignalCalculator.java:22 throws raw IllegalStateException; RunSignalCalculator.java:24 bare .orElseThrow() -> convert to SystemRuntimeErrorException(SystemMessage.error(RESOURCE_NOT_FOUND)) for consistency. Verified mitigation: both sit behind a same-transaction lookup of a just-saved row (SportService.logSportSession:66-68, RunningService.logSession:121-126) so a client cannot practically trigger them - style consistency, not a leak. (d) Query batching: WorkoutService.skipExercise runs findByCreatedByAndWorkoutSessionIdOrderByCreatedAtAsc TWICE (:224 and :230) - fetch once; WorkoutService.saveFeedback (:246-265) does per-item findById + findByCreatedByAndWorkoutSessionIdAndExerciseId + save in a loop (2N+N) - batch-load + saveAll; TrainService.replaceDayExercises:188 calls save() in a for loop -> saveAll. (e) Add a techcore ownedByIdOrThrow(repo, id, userId) helper (or equivalent) replacing the ~10 copy-pasted findById().filter(createdBy.equals()).orElseThrow(notFound) gates across TrainService/WorkoutService - one tested home for the foreign-row==404 invariant. IMPORTANT refuted idea, do NOT pursue: widening OwnedRepository across ~25 repos - it only offers a date-ordered findAllOwned; several repos deliberately cannot extend it (no date base field; documented at PantryItemRepository.java:9, RecipeRepository.java:9, ExerciseSetRepository.java:10, MesocycleRepository.java:11). (f) Optional low-prio: ExerciseRecordService .limit(3)/.limit(5) output shapers (:120,:125) -> *Properties record per configuration_conventions.md.
notes: Done, merged 7ae1710, suite green (432/0) on Testcontainers path. IMPORTANT learning: the audit was wrong that all readOnly reads were safe to strip — RecipeService.get/list, MealService.recipeLogs, FuelDayService.getDay traverse LAZY associations and NEED @Transactional(readOnly=true) with open-in-view:false (6 ITs went 500 without it). Rule exception documented in spring_patterns.md tx table + commented at each site. OwnershipGuard (techcore/persistence) now hosts ownedOrThrow + canonical notFound(). Skipped as agreed: (f) ExerciseRecordService limit magic numbers (optional, low value).

title: Backend: resolve train <-> progression package cycle
description: Verified two-way package dependency (java_package_structure.md forbids feature-package cycles): train services import feature.progression.* (WorkoutService.java:14-18, RunningService.java:7-11, SportService.java:7-11) while progression classes import feature.train entities/repositories (SportSignalCalculator.java:3-4, GymSignalCalculator.java:3-8, RunSignalCalculator.java:3-7, RobustnessCalculator.java:3-5). No Spring BEAN cycle exists - the calculators inject train repositories (Spring Data beans), not train services, so the bean graph is acyclic and the app wires fine; this is a package-structure/separation issue, not a runtime bug. Options: (a) move the signal calculators into feature/train (they read train data and emit a signal), keeping the dependency arrow train -> progression only; or (b) introduce a one-directional seam: train publishes a signal/event, progression consumes it. After the fix, add the no-cycle rule to the ArchUnit issue (same epic) to freeze it.

Chosen seam: (a)+dependency-inversion hybrid — 3 signal calculators moved to feature/train/signal/ (git mv, history kept); Signal records stay progression-side as the one-way contract; RobustnessCalculator replaced by progression-side RobustnessSource PORT + train-side TrainingStreakCalculator impl. title: [bug] MedicationApiIT.testLogDose_…_whenPostedToday flaky: fails before 07:00 UTC
description: testLogDose_shouldReturn201AndStartCycle_whenPostedToday sets administeredAt to LocalDate.now(UTC).atTime(7,0). When the suite runs before 07:00 UTC (09:00 CEST), that timestamp is in the future and the medication future-date guard returns 400, failing the test. Time-of-day-dependent flake from mezo-d94. Fix: use a today timestamp that is never in the future (start-of-day UTC), preserving the 'today → retaDay 1' semantics. Surfaced while running P3b (mezo-lmox) gates at 04:46 UTC.
notes: Fixed in f64fa89: administeredAt now start-of-day UTC, never future.

title: Fix iOS overscroll/rubber-band at top of the app scroller
description: On installed PWA / iOS Safari the user can drag the page downward at the very top, rubber-banding the .screen-content scroller (and chaining to the viewport), revealing empty space. Root cause: .screen-content (the single app scroller) has no overscroll-behavior set, so the browser default permits the boundary bounce + scroll-chain. Fix: overscroll-behavior-y:none + -webkit-overflow-scrolling:touch on .screen-content, plus overscroll-behavior:none on html,body as belt-and-suspenders. CSS-only, frontend/src/styles/prototype.css.

title: Quarantine FE mock seed to mock mode: real-mode dual-mode hooks must never fall back to the static seed (cold-load flash)
description: Root-cause class behind mezo-yew. Four dual-mode hooks use a default-param fallback to the STATIC mock seed that also fires in REAL mode while the query is loading (initialData is undefined in real mode, so data is undefined until resolve → the '= mockSeed' default kicks in): pantryHooks.ts:16 (= mockData), recipeHooks.ts:17 (= mockRecipes), fuelHooks.ts:33 (= seedDayData), weightHooks.ts:37 (= mockWeightTrends). Effect: on a cold load / hard refresh in real mode the user briefly sees FAKE seed data (mock recipes/pantry/day/weight-trend) before their real data loads. Daniel always tests live with his own data, so he hits this flash. The '= []' hooks (weightLog, sleepLog, goals) are already safe (empty, not seed).
design: Invariant: REAL mode NEVER returns mock seed — unresolved query → an EMPTY/zero value (or a loading skeleton), never the seed. Recommended: a shared useDualQuery<T>(key, { mock, mockData, realFetch, realEmpty }) helper that bakes in initialData: mock ? mockData : undefined, staleTime: mock ? Infinity : 0, and data: q.data ?? (mock ? mockData : realEmpty). Migrate the 4 leaking hooks (+ optionally the safe ones for consistency) to it; pass a realEmpty per hook ({ingredients:[],stash:[]} / [] / zero-targets FuelDay / neutral WeightTrends). Components already tolerate empty (Kamra/recipe empty states). Optional follow-on: expose isPending so views can show skeletons instead of an empty flash. Keep mock-mode behavior byte-identical (synchronous initialData for parity/tests).
close_reason: Shipped: useDualQuery helper enforces 'real mode never returns the mock seed' (realEmpty, never seed during the loading window). Migrated usePantry/useRecipes/useFuelDay/useWeight(trends); already-safe hooks untouched. Durable guard test (dualMode.guard.test.ts) fails the build if the leaky '{ data = seed } = useQuery' pattern reappears anywhere in src (sibling-tolerant regex). Shared pct() 0/0->0 helper (MacroHero + LogMealSheet). bd memory dual-mode-read-invariant saved. Adversarial review clean after 2 fix rounds. FE 605/605 both modes, build clean. Merged 0d70a7f, deployed.

title: Recipe-arm meal-log contribution rounding: converge backend double-round vs FE single-round
description: Meal-logging (mezo-arb): a recipe-arm meal_item's displayed contribution can be ±1-3 kcal off the persisted value. Backend DOUBLE-rounds (MealService.perServing rounds whole/servings to scale 0 -> snapshot_kcal whole, then MealMapper round(snapshot×amount)), while the FE single-rounds (fuelHooks buildLine + LogMealSheet round((whole/servings)×amount)). E.g. 1000kcal/3 servings ×2 -> backend 666, FE 667. Cosmetic + self-correcting (getDay refetch repaints the server value) but mock!=real on the recipe arm. Converge all 3 sites onto ONE convention (recommend: drop the backend per-serving scale-0 round so the snapshot keeps precision -> single-round everywhere = the more accurate value; OR double-round the FE to match the stored whole-number snapshot). Pantry arm is already exact parity.
close_reason: MealService.perServing keeps the per-serving snapshot at full precision (scale 6) so contribution rounds once at the scaled() boundary — converges the recipe arm with the FE single-round; pantry arm untouched. Repro IT (297/2 @ 2 adag → 297, was 298) green, full suite 382/0/0. fuel.md parity note updated.

title: Run builder: reconcile weeks scalar with structure.weeks.length (single source + mock fixtures)
description: Follow-up from Task 7 code review (mezo-9pv). RunningBlockBuilder tracks draft.weeks (scalar) and draft.structure.weeks.length independently; addWeek/removeLastWeek + the Math.min/max(8/1) clamp keep them in sync ONLY when they start synced. Real-backend data is always consistent (newDraft builds N weeks; create/update persist the full structure), so production is unaffected. But the Phase-1 mock fixtures are inconsistent: running.ts rb-archived-01 (weeks:8 / structure.weeks:1) and rb-planned-01 (weeks:6 / structure.weeks:1) — opening them in mock mode renders N week chips of which all but week 1 show 'Ez a hét nincs a tervben'. Fix: (1) make the mock fixtures consistent (structure.weeks.length === weeks), and (2) derive the week-chip count + add/remove gates from draft.structure.weeks.length as the single source of truth, recomputing the weeks scalar from the structure on save, so the counter/gates/editor can never disagree. Also minor a11y: give the auto-save indicator role=status/aria-live=polite. Not blocking the redesign merge.

title: [bug] Recipe editor: a line for a since-deleted pantry item shows UUID + 0 macros (seed snapshot name/contribution into DraftLine)
description: Pre-existing (predates mezo-yew, surfaced by the adversarial review of that fix). In RecipeEditorView edit mode, a recipe line whose pantry item was soft-deleted no longer resolves against usePantry().ingredients → row renders line.refId (UUID) + contributionOf(line, undefined)=0, and zero-drags the live MAKRO-OSSZEG. The data to fix this already exists: the backend persists snapshotName + snapshot macros on recipe_ingredient, and recipeApi.fromResponse carries them onto RecipeIngredientLine.name/.contribution — but the editor's DraftLine seed (RecipeEditorView.tsx ~line 79) discards both. Fix: carry name (and contribution) into DraftLine, and fall back to the snapshot name when usePantry() resolution misses; the live editor cannot recompute contributions for a missing ingredient, so for a deleted item show the frozen snapshot contribution at the stored amount (and consider disabling the stepper for that orphan line). Low priority: requires soft-deleting a pantry item still referenced by a saved recipe, no data corruption (amounts/refIds preserved on save) — only a misleading display until re-pick.

Git workflow (single-dev --no-ff): run 'git pull --rebase' BEFORE 'git merge --no-ff <branch>', NOT in the push step after. The Session-Completion 'git pull --rebase && bd dolt push && git push' sequence, when run after a local --no-ff merge, replays the merge+close commits and FLATTENS the --no-ff bubble into linear history (happened on mezo-ksh/dh6/8xy/cki — work correct + pushed, but bubbles lost). Correct order: checkout main → pull --rebase → merge --no-ff → push (no second pull). If origin truly moved before push, use 'git pull --rebase=merges' to preserve the bubble.

Hibernate 7.1.x typed jsonb (@JdbcTypeCode(SqlTypes.JSON)) requires com.fasterxml.jackson.core:jackson-databind (Jackson 2) on the backend classpath - Hibernate 7.1.8 ships only the Jackson2-bound JacksonJsonFormatMapper, no tools.jackson (Jackson 3) FormatMapper. SB4 MVC serializes HTTP with Jackson 3; the two majors coexist in disjoint code paths. Do NOT remove the jackson-databind 2.x dependency from backend/pom.xml or jsonb persistence breaks (context fails: 'Could not find a FormatMapper for the JSON format'). Re-evaluate when Hibernate ships a Jackson 3 mapper. Established in mezo-n5q (Slice B, ProvenanceEnvelope); Fuel slice meal-score jsonb relies on the same pattern.

db8 hit a real bootstrap failure: MealRepository extends OwnedRepository<MealEntity> (brief-mandated for the date-ordered family), but the inherited findAllOwned JPQL orders by e.date — a field MealEntity lacks (it has loggedAt/mealDate). On Spring Boot 4 / Hibernate 7, derived+@Query queries are VALIDATED EAGERLY at context startup, so the invalid inherited query fails bean creation ('Query validation failed for select e from MealEntity e ... order by e.date asc') even though nothing calls it. The brief assumed an uncalled-but-inherited invalid query is harmless; under eager validation it is not. FIX faithful to brief intent: override findAllOwned in MealRepository with a @Query valid for MealEntity (order by mealDate asc, loggedAt asc), keeping 'extends OwnedRepository' exactly as mandated. The override stays unused by the service (it uses the day-finder) — it exists only to give the family a MealEntity-valid query.

macOS git-hook gotcha: GNU 'timeout' is NOT installed on this machine, so the beads pre-commit/pre-push hooks run 'bd hooks run' WITHOUT a timeout guard. If multiple bd processes contend for the Dolt lock (e.g. bd init's own commit colliding with manual git commits), the hook hangs INDEFINITELY and git commit/push silently stalls. Fix when it happens: kill the stuck 'bd hooks run' + 'bd export' processes (ps aux | grep bd) to clear the lock, then retry — a single serialized commit works fine. Permanent fix: brew install coreutils and put its gnubin on PATH so 'timeout' exists.

SB4/Spring Security 7 backend gotchas (found in Slice-A auth): (1) TestRestTemplate moved to org.springframework.boot.resttestclient.TestRestTemplate (artifact spring-boot-resttestclient, transitive via spring-boot-starter-webmvc-test) and requires @AutoConfigureTestRestTemplate to register the bean under RANDOM_PORT. (2) NimbusJwtEncoder cannot infer the JWS alg from a symmetric ImmutableSecret -> must pass JwsHeader.with(MacAlgorithm.HS256) via JwtEncoderParameters.from(header, claims), else 'Failed to select a JWK signing key'. (3) JWT resource server needs spring-boot-starter-oauth2-resource-server (not in Initializr defaults; brings Nimbus JOSE). (4) The plan's SecurityConfig DSL (csrf(c->c.disable()), oauth2ResourceServer(o->o.jwt(j->{}))) is valid as-is on SS7. (5) SB4 ships Jackson 3: packages are tools.jackson.databind.ObjectMapper/JsonNode (NOT com.fasterxml.* — only jackson-annotations 2.x remains under com.fasterxml); JsonNode.asText() is deprecated, use asString(); exceptions are unchecked. (6) openapi-generator 7.17.0 'spring' generator works on SB4 with useSpringBoot4=true + useResponseEntity=false (emits @ResponseStatus from the first 2xx code, @Valid on interface params); generated com.fasterxml @JsonProperty annotations are honored by Jackson 3. Contract gotchas: NotBlank has no OpenAPI equivalent (required + minLength:1 -> Size -> VALIDATION_INVALID_VALUE, not REQUIRED_FIELD); prefer pattern over enum on request fields (invalid enum fails Jackson deser -> 500, pattern fails bean validation -> 400). IDE language server does not index target/generated-sources/openapi -> api.dto/api.controller 'cannot be resolved' diags are noise, Maven is truth.

DUAL-MODE READ INVARIANT (mezo-0xl): real mode must NEVER return the FE mock seed — not even transiently during the query's loading window. The footgun is 'const { data = mockSeed } = useQuery(...)': the destructuring default fires whenever data is undefined, which INCLUDES the entire real-mode load, so it flashes Phase-1 demo data onto a live user's screen (this is the same bug class as mezo-yew). ALWAYS use the useDualQuery helper (frontend/src/data/useDualQuery.ts) for a dual-mode read whose mock side is a static literal: useDualQuery({ queryKey, mockData, realFetch, realEmpty, realStaleTime? }) returns data: q.data ?? (mock ? mockData : realEmpty) — real mode gets realEmpty (empty/zero), never the seed; mock keeps synchronous initialData + staleTime Infinity for parity. An empty-literal default (= [] / = {}) is also safe. A guard test (frontend/src/data/dualMode.guard.test.ts) FAILS THE BUILD if the leaky '{ data = seed } = useQuery' pattern reappears in any src/data hook. Migrated hooks: usePantry ({ingredients:[],stash:[]}), useRecipes ([]), useFuelDay (zero FuelDay), useWeight weightTrends arm (zero trend). Already-safe hooks (train/running/goal/biometric/sleep + weightLog) use '?? []'/'?? null'/isPending and were left as-is. Consumer corollary: components dividing by a target/count must guard 0/0 (MacroHero now does → 0%), because realEmpty can be a zero value. Canonical doc: docs/features/_platform-data-layer.md §'The no static fallback in real mode rule' + the useDualQuery paragraph in §4.

Sheet CLOSE animation gotcha: setting el.style.animation='none' + transition + transform in ONE sync tick makes the browser coalesce the writes and JUMP to the end state (instant disappear) instead of animating — Chromium-headless tolerated it but real browsers (e.g. Safari) did not. FIX in requestClose(): animation='none'; void el.offsetHeight (force reflow to commit current pos as start frame); set transition; then set transform:translateY(100%) inside requestAnimationFrame (next frame). Verified gradual ease-out curve 0->460px over ~300ms. Entrance slide-up is a pure CSS @keyframes (sheet-rise) so it never had this problem. LESSON: when verifying animations in Playwright, sample the transform at MULTIPLE timestamps to confirm a gradual curve — a single mid-sample at full height can mask an instant jump (it fooled me once).

DEPLOY GOTCHA: never put [skip ci] on the HEAD commit of a push to main — GitHub natively skips the whole workflow run when the push's head commit message contains [skip ci]. The deploy.yml triggers on 'push: branches:[main]' (no paths filter) and the version job also has if:!contains(head_commit.message,'[skip ci]'). Project convention: bd-chore/close commits normally do NOT carry [skip ci] (they deploy fine). When merging a slice + closing its bd issue, make the LAST pushed commit a non-[skip ci] one (the merge commit, or a clean trigger), otherwise the deploy is suppressed. Sheet (src/components/ui/Sheet.tsx) architecture & behavior: renders via createPortal into '.phone-screen' (fallback document.body) so position:absolute;bottom:0 anchors to the DEVICE viewport and the backdrop covers the tab bar — NOT the scrolling .screen-content it is mounted inside (original bug: CheckInSheet rendered inside scroll content, so the sheet sat mid-page). Entrance = pure CSS @keyframes sheet-rise (slide up) — reliable. Drag-to-dismiss on .sheet-handle-zone (close if dy>min(120px,28% height) OR velocity>0.5px/ms; else snap back). CLOSE is animated on EVERY path (backdrop, Escape, drag, in-sheet X/Save): requestClose() slides down + fades backdrop ~300ms then calls onClose. The animated close reaches children via RENDER-PROP: children may be (close)=>ReactNode; CheckInSheet & QuickInputSheet use {(close)=>(...)} and call close() from X/Save/commit. CONSEQUENCE: onClose is ASYNC now — tests asserting closure must use waitFor (Sheet.test, QuickInputSheet.test, navigation.test). The close depends on the reflow/rAF trick in memory key sheet-close-animation-gotcha-setting-el-style-animation.
